item.title: 14 people charged with 43 counts of sexual offences in the May, 9 incidents where the victims and the accused were related to one another

item.title: Tukana supports Grace Road development at Princes Road while Compain says success in business does not place anyone above the law

item.title: NFP rules out pre-election coalition deals for 2026 polls
Applications open for Prime Minister’s International Business Awards 2026
item.description: Applications for the Prime Minister’s International Business Awards (PMIBA) 2026 are now open, with businesses given three months to apply for what is being described as the country’s most prestigious business award. Investment Fiji Chief Executive Officer Kamal Chetty says winning an award can help businesses access new markets, motivate employees and gain additional support.
item.content:encoded:
Applications for the Prime Minister’s International Business Awards (PMIBA) 2026 are now open, with businesses given three months to apply for what is being described as the country’s most prestigious business award.

Investment Fiji Chief Executive Officer Kamal Chetty says winning an award can help businesses access new markets, motivate employees and gain additional support.

Chetty says last year’s awards were a great success, attracting the highest number of attendees and a significant increase in applications.

He says this year’s awards align with the theme, "Economic Transformation, Inclusive Growth", and are designed to recognise and encourage businesses contributing to Fiji’s economic development and transformation.

He adds that award winners will be given priority when Investment Fiji identifies international opportunities and explores new markets.

Chetty says applicants must be Fiji-registered companies and tax compliant.

He says there are 16 categories this year, including three general categories and several special categories.

He says the Supreme Award will be selected from among the category winners and does not require a separate application.

Meanwhile, PMIBA Working Committee Chair Leilani Volau says applications are being accepted entirely online through IBA Fiji.

Volau says businesses must create an account, verify their email address and complete the online application form.

She says all companies are required to enter a general category and may also apply for any relevant special categories.

Volau says applicants can save their progress and return later to complete and submit them.

She says organisers are aiming to increase both applications and attendance after last year’s event attracted 760 attendees.

She adds that the application drive team will be available to assist businesses with any queries regarding the application process or award categories.

Applications will remain open until August, while the awards ceremony is expected to be held in November. The venue is yet to be confirmed.

item.title: Sundar pleads not guilty of killing and dismembering his grandfather
item.description: The man accused of killing his grandfather and dismembering his body in Lutu Village, Wainibuka last year has pleaded not guilty. Charles Laxton Sundar is charged with one count of murder.
item.content:encoded:
The man accused of killing his grandfather and dismembering his body in Lutu Village, Wainibuka last year has pleaded not guilty.

Charles Laxton Sundar is charged with one count of murder.

Suva High Court Judge Justice Usaia Ratuvili had found Sundar fit to stand trial following a psychiatric evaluation.

It is alleged that Sundar slit his grandfather’s throat and dismembered his body with a caneknife on the 12th of July last year.

The Office of the Director of Public Prosecutions has told court that Sundar confessed during his caution interview and the defense counsel will not be challenging it.

The matter has been adjourned to the 22nd of June for a pre-trial conference .

item.title: Court orders retrial in Rasova's FICAC case
item.description: The Suva Magistrates Court has ordered a new trial in former SODELPA Member of Parliament, Simione Rasova’s FICAC case. The court

item.title: Beqa women turn passion for floriculture into growing income opportunity
item.description: A women's group in Beqa is continuing to grow its floriculture business with support from the Ministry of Agriculture's Women in Agriculture Programme. The Dakuni Women's Group, led by Adi Sala Vibote, received assistance through the programme during the 2024/2025 financial year and has been developing its anthurium project for the past three years.
item.content:encoded:
A women's group in Beqa is continuing to grow its floriculture business with support from the Ministry of Agriculture's Women in Agriculture Programme.

The Dakuni Women's Group, led by Adi Sala Vibote, received assistance through the programme during the 2024/2025 financial year and has been developing its anthurium project for the past three years.

The Ministry of Agriculture says the group currently has 20 active members who manage 20 healthy anthurium plants in a greenhouse using practical growing methods.

They say Vibote's passion for floriculture has inspired other women in the village to learn new skills and take part in agriculture.

The Ministry adds that the assistance has helped the group maintain its greenhouse, explore other plant varieties and strengthen women's participation in the agricultural sector.

item.description: The Fiji Airways Men's National 7s team secured a hard-fought 15-12 victory over South Africa in their final pool match, surviving
